title: Machine-Verified SQG Shear-Vorticity Identity + Conditional Theorem 3 Roadmap (Lean 4)
description: Lean 4 + mathlib formalization of Fourier-space identities for the Surface Quasi-Geostrophic equation, working towards a machine-checked regularity proof. Covers Theorems 1 and 2 of paper D14 (shear-vorticity identity Ŝ_nt - ω̂/2 = |k|·sin²(α-β)·θ̂ and selection-rule bound) plus supporting Riesz/Sobolev infrastructure on the 2-torus. §10 adds a conditional Theorem 3 (SQG regularity) roadmap with named axiomatic hypotheses — MaterialMaxPrinciple, BKMCriterion, FracSobolevCalculus, SqgWellPosedness — pinning down exactly which analytic facts the regularity argument borrows from outside the algebraic layer. FracSobolevCalculus is discharged unconditionally. BKM's axiomatic scope is reduced first to s > 1 (§10.6 interpolation) and then further to the integer-order range s ∈ (1, 2] only (§10.8 S2 bootstrap, BKMCriterionS2) — which avoids fractional calculus entirely by using only classical differential commutators. MaterialMaxPrinciple alone handles s ∈ [0, 1], and the combined conditional Theorem 3 on s ∈ [0, 2] rests on integer-order axioms only. v0.4.1 adds §10.9 Fourier convolution scaffolding (`fourierConvolution`, `convolution_bounded_by_product` with the uniform Young bound), §10.10 mode-Lipschitz keystone upgrade (`ModeLipschitz`, `SqgEvolutionAxioms_strong`), and §10.11 SQG-specific Bochner wiring (`DuhamelFlux`, `DuhamelFlux.modeLipschitz`, `SqgEvolutionAxioms.strengthen_of_duhamel`) — giving a fully machine-checked path from 'bounded per-mode flux' (via `convolution_bounded_by_product`) through `DuhamelFlux → ModeLipschitz → SqgEvolutionAxioms_strong` to the §10.7 / §10.8 reductions. The only remaining analytic step is a real-SQG-solution witness for `DuhamelFlux`. All content machine-verified with zero sorry across 7746 lines. Mathematical theorems by Bryan Sanchez (independent researcher); Lean formalization by Bryan Sanchez with AI assistance.
